templates.text: '{quizQuestion}' — you answered '{echoText}'. Even the dungeon pauses at that.
The question lingers: {quizQuestion} The answer — {echoText} — carved itself into the wall before you could speak.
They asked that same question here, long ago. '{quizQuestion}' The answer was always {echoText}.
The walls have been asking '{quizQuestion}' for years. You are the first to answer correctly. They were beginning to doubt the question was answerable.
The dungeon knew the question. It did not know if you knew the answer. '{quizQuestion}' — and then: {echoText}. The uncertainty resolves.
Both are inscribed somewhere in the lower levels: '{quizQuestion}' and, beneath it, '{echoText}'. You have caught up to an inscription that was waiting for you.
'{quizQuestion}' A question with a history in this place. '{echoText}' — an answer with a future. The dungeon files them together.
'{echoText}.' The answer to '{quizQuestion}' — carried through three floors of stone to reach the question. The resonance between them is audible, if you know what to listen for.
